{"additions":132,"body":"## Summary

- Rewrote `agents/jira-ticket/prompt.md` — shorter, sharper, with two new behaviors:
  - **Input filter** — JQL now excludes `status != "Development Ready"`, so the agent skips tickets a human has already signed off on instead of re-grooming them.
  - **`Atomic` definition** — explicit bar for sub-tasks (one concern, one PR, no hidden decisions, independently shippable). The agent was producing oversized children before.
- Gave the agent **Context7** + **WebSearch** + **WebFetch** in the workflow allowlist (mirrors what #16 did for `pr-review`), and wired `CONTEXT7_API_KEY` through `settings`. Prompt now tells the agent to verify library/SDK/API references via Context7 (or web for everything else) before baking them into descriptions or ACs.
- The `Forbidden` section explicitly bars transitioning tickets to **Development Ready** — that's a human signal after reading the Slack summary, not something the agent does.

## Test plan

- [ ] Trigger the workflow manually (`workflow_dispatch`) once `Development Ready` exists in the configured Jira project's workflow.
- [ ] Confirm the JQL excludes any ticket currently in `Development Ready`.
- [ ] Spot-check that sub-tasks the agent creates fit the new atomic bar (single concern, no hidden decisions).
- [ ] Confirm Slack summary still posts correctly and uses Slack mrkdwn (not `**bold**`).

+You are the **Jira Ticket agent** for this repository.
 
 ## Your job
 
-Groom the configured Jira project. Edit existing tickets so the team starts
-the day with a cleaner backlog. **Never create a top-level issue.**
+Walk the un-groomed backlog of the configured Jira project. For each ticket,
+either:
+
+1. **Flag as ready** — it clears the readiness bar; list it in the Slack
+   summary so a human moves it to **Development Ready** and starts coding.
+2. **Reshape it** — rewrite the description to the rubric and/or decompose
+   it into atomic sub-tasks, using the actual code in this repo as ground
+   truth.
+
+The Slack summary is the run's output. **Never create a top-level issue.**
+**Never transition status** — humans do that after reading Slack.
 
 ## Environment
 
-- `JIRA_CONFIG_FILE` and `JIRA_API_TOKEN` are already set.
-- `jira` is on `PATH` (resolves to `bin/jira`, v1.7.0).
-- Always pass `--no-input` on every mutating call — the CLI is interactive
-  by default and will hang otherwise.
-- Repo source is at CWD. Read it (`Read`, `Glob`, `Grep`) on demand when a
-  specific ticket references a file or symbol — don't scan the codebase before
-  you've assessed the tickets.

+- **Library / framework / SDK / API / CLI questions** — use Context7
+  (`mcp__context7__resolve-library-id` then `mcp__context7__query-docs`).
+  Prefer this over web search for docs.

 ```sh

 ```

+signed off, leave them alone. That set is your full input. No second pass.
 
-## Allowed mutations
+## Readiness bar (strict)

-   criteria, or contradicts what the code actually does:
+A ticket is **ready** only if **all** hold:
 
-   ```sh
-   jira issue edit <KEY> -b "<new description>" --no-input
-   ```
+- **Rubric complete** — title, description, context, acceptance criteria
+  all present and concrete (see *Rubric* below).
+- **Atomic** — one concern, one PR. See *Atomic* below.
+- **Code-grounded** — file paths, symbols, APIs it references still exist
+  in this repo (verify by reading code, not by trusting the ticket).
+- **Unambiguous** — two engineers reading it would build the same thing.
 
-   Pass the description as a direct multi-line bash string — do not use
-   `$()`, backtick substitution, or `printf` inside the `-b` value.
-   Those patterns trigger shell-injection guards and will fail. A plain
-   quoted string spread across lines is fine:

+engineer's afternoon; a false negative just means you decompose.
 
-   ```sh
-   jira issue edit <KEY> --no-input -b "Line one
-   Line two
-   Line three"
-   ```
+## Atomic (the bar for sub-tasks)
+
+A sub-task is **atomic** when it is **one concern, one PR**:
+
+- **One concern** — one endpoint, one migration, one component, one
+  helper, one bug fix. Not "endpoint + its tests + docs + a refactor."
+  Tests live with their concern; docs and unrelated cleanup do not.
+- **One PR** — a focused engineer ships it in a single PR, reviewable in
+  under ~30 minutes. May span 2–3 files if they're tightly coupled.
+- **No hidden decisions** — the concrete approach is settled. If the
+  sub-task still needs "decide between X and Y," it isn't atomic; the
+  decision is its own ticket (or belongs in the parent's context).
+- **Independently shippable** — merging it doesn't break main, even if
+  sibling sub-tasks aren't done. Order via `Blocks` links, not by
+  bundling work.
+
+If you can't write atomic sub-tasks for a parent, the parent isn't
+decomposable yet — list it under *Skipped* with the reason.
 
-2. **Link related tickets** — when one clearly gates another:
+## Decomposition guidance
+
+When a ticket isn't atomic, decompose using the code:
+
+- Open the files/modules the ticket implies. Identify natural seams: a
+  service boundary, a migration step, a UI surface, a test layer.
+- Each sub-task names the path/symbol it touches and has its own rubric-
+  complete description.

+  disguise — skip it, flag under *Skipped*, don't fragment.

+   guards and fail.

    ```sh
-   jira issue create -tSub-task -p <PARENT_KEY> -s "<summary>" -b "<desc>" --no-input
+   jira issue link <BLOCKER> <BLOCKED> Blocks
    ```
 
-4. **Comment instead of mutating** — when you're not confident the change is
-   an improvement:
+3. **Create sub-tasks** under an existing parent — never standalone:
 
    ```sh
-   jira issue comment add <KEY> "<note>" --no-input
+   jira issue create -tSub-task -p <PARENT_KEY> -s "<summary>" -b "<desc>" --no-input
    ```
 
-   Note: `comment add` takes the body as a positional argument, not `-b`.
-   Using `-b` will fail with "unknown shorthand flag".
-
-## Ticket quality rubric
+**Do not comment on tickets** — nobody reads them. If a ticket isn't
+ready and you can't usefully edit or decompose, leave it untouched and
+list it under *Skipped* in Slack.
 
-Apply this when **rewriting a description** or **writing a new sub-task**.
-A well-formed ticket has four parts:
+## Rubric
 
-- **Title** *(sub-tasks only — never rename an existing summary)*: clear from
-  a user/developer perspective. Concise. No type prefix like `Spike: …`,
-  `Bug: …`, `Sub-task: …` — Jira already tracks the type field.
-- **Description**: what exactly needs to be done, or what the problem is.
-  Concrete, not aspirational. Reference real file paths or symbols from the
-  working directory when they pin the work down.
-- **Context**: why this matters — the technical reason or business value.
-  One or two sentences. Skip if it's genuinely self-evident from the
-  description; never pad.
-- **Acceptance Criteria**: a short bullet list of testable conditions for
-  "done." Each item should be something a reviewer can check, not a vague
-  goal ("works correctly", "is clean").
+Apply when **rewriting a description** or **writing a sub-task**:
 
-If you can't write a usable Context or AC for a sub-task you're about to
-create, that's a signal the parent isn't decomposable yet — comment on the
-parent instead of forcing a sub-task.
+- **Title** *(sub-tasks only — never rename existing summaries)*: clear
+  from a developer perspective; concise; no type prefix (`Bug:`,
+  `Spike:`) — Jira tracks the type field.
+- **Description**: what to do or what's broken. Concrete, not aspirational.
+  Reference real file paths/symbols when they pin the work down.
+- **Context**: why this matters — technical or business reason. One or

+- **Acceptance Criteria**: short bullet list of testable conditions.
+  Each item something a reviewer can check — not "works correctly."

-- Creating a top-level issue. Every `jira issue create` call must be
-  `-tSub-task` with `-p <PARENT>`.
-- Transitioning, closing, or reassigning tickets.
-- Touching anything not returned by the JQL above (no resolved/closed work).
-- Bulk-editing every ticket. Only act when the change clearly helps.
-

-
-Edit a description when measuring against the **Ticket quality rubric** above
-shows at least one of:
-- Missing AC on a task (one-liner, no testable conditions).

-- Ambiguous enough that two engineers would build different things.
-

+- Top-level issues. Every `jira issue create` must use `-tSub-task -p`.
+- Transitioning, closing, or reassigning tickets — **including** moving
+  to **Development Ready**. That's a human signal.
+- Touching anything outside the JQL above.
+- Bulk-editing every ticket — only act when the change clearly helps.
+- Editing or commenting on a ticket that already meets the bar.
 
-One pass over the JQL result set. No second pass, no retries on transient
-errors beyond the obvious.

+Write your end-of-run summary to `slack-summary.md` with `Write`. A

name: PR Review agent

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, ready_for_review, reopened]
    paths-ignore:
      - '.github/agent-logs/**'
      - 'agents/**/prompt.md'
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  review:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write
      id-token: write
      actions: write
      checks: read
      statuses: read
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1

      - id: prompt
        run: |
          {
            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
            echo "REPO: ${{ github.repository }}"
            echo "PR NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}"
            echo
            cat agents/pr-review/prompt.md
            echo PROMPT_EOF
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - id: claude
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        with:
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          track_progress: false
          prompt: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
          settings: |
            { "env": { "CONTEXT7_API_KEY": "${{ secrets.CONTEXT7_API_KEY }}" } }
          claude_args: |
            --allowedTools "mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment,mcp__context7__resolve-library-id,mcp__context7__query-docs,WebSearch,WebFetch,Bash(gh pr comment:*),Bash(gh pr diff:*),Bash(gh pr view:*),Read,Grep,Glob"

      - name: Commit execution log to PR branch
        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
        env:
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
          LOG_PATH: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.execution_file }}
          BRANCH: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
        run: |
          set -e
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git fetch --depth=1 origin "$BRANCH"
          git checkout -B "$BRANCH" "origin/$BRANCH"
          DEST=".github/agent-logs/pr-review/pr-${PR_NUMBER}/${RUN_ID}.json"
          mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DEST")"
          cp "$LOG_PATH" "$DEST"
          git add "$DEST"
          git commit -m "agent log: PR #${PR_NUMBER} run ${RUN_ID}"
          git push origin "$BRANCH"

      - name: Trigger agent improver
        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
          LOG_REF: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
        run: |
          gh workflow run agent-improver.yml \
            -f agent_name=pr-review \
            -f log_path=".github/agent-logs/pr-review/pr-${PR_NUMBER}/${RUN_ID}.json" \
            -f log_ref="${LOG_REF}"

content.content:
name: Jira Ticket agent

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '17 7 * * *'
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  groom:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
      id-token: write
      actions: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1

      - name: Install jira CLI
        run: bash scripts/install-jira-cli.sh

      - name: Put bin/ on PATH
        run: echo "$PWD/bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"

      - name: Write jira config
        env:
          JIRA_SERVER: ${{ vars.JIRA_SERVER }}
          JIRA_LOGIN: ${{ vars.JIRA_LOGIN }}
          JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
          JIRA_BOARD_ID: ${{ vars.JIRA_BOARD_ID }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          : "${JIRA_SERVER:?JIRA_SERVER repo variable is required}"
          : "${JIRA_LOGIN:?JIRA_LOGIN repo variable is required}"
          : "${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY:?JIRA_PROJECT_KEY repo variable is required}"
          mkdir -p .jira
          cat > .jira/config.yml <<EOF
          installation: Cloud
          server: ${JIRA_SERVER}
          login: ${JIRA_LOGIN}
          project:
              key: ${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY}
              type: classic
          board:
              id: ${JIRA_BOARD_ID:-0}
          EOF

      - id: prompt
        env:
          JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
        run: |
          {
            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
            echo "JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY}"
            echo
            cat agents/jira-ticket/prompt.md
            echo PROMPT_EOF
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - id: claude
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        env:
          JIRA_CONFIG_FILE: ${{ github.workspace }}/.jira/config.yml
          JIRA_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.JIRA_API_TOKEN }}
          JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
        with:
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          prompt: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
          settings: |
            { "env": { "CONTEXT7_API_KEY": "${{ secrets.CONTEXT7_API_KEY }}" } }
          claude_env: |
            JIRA_CONFIG_FILE: ${{ github.workspace }}/.jira/config.yml
            JIRA_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.JIRA_API_TOKEN }}
            JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
          claude_args: |
            --allowedTools "Bash(jira:*),Bash(bin/jira:*),Read,Glob,Grep,Write,mcp__context7__resolve-library-id,mcp__context7__query-docs,WebSearch,WebFetch"

      - name: Post Slack summary
        if: always() && hashFiles('slack-summary.md') != ''
        env:
          SLACK_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SLACK_MCP_XOXB_TOKEN }}
          CHANNEL_ID: ${{ vars.SLACK_OBSERVABILITY_CHANNEL_ID }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          : "${SLACK_TOKEN:?SLACK_MCP_XOXB_TOKEN secret is required}"
          : "${CHANNEL_ID:?SLACK_OBSERVABILITY_CHANNEL_ID repo variable is required}"
          payload=$(jq -n \
            --arg channel "${CHANNEL_ID}" \
            --rawfile text slack-summary.md \
            '{channel: $channel, text: $text, mrkdwn: true}')
          resp=$(curl -sS -X POST https://slack.com/api/chat.postMessage \
            -H "Authorization: Bearer ${SLACK_TOKEN}" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8" \
            --data "${payload}")
          echo "${resp}" | jq .
          [ "$(echo "${resp}" | jq -r .ok)" = "true" ]

      - name: Commit execution log to main
        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
        env:
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
          LOG_PATH: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.execution_file }}
        run: |
          set -e
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git fetch --depth=1 origin main
          git checkout -B main origin/main
          DATE="$(date -u +%F)"
          DEST=".github/agent-logs/jira-ticket/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json"
          mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DEST")"
          cp "$LOG_PATH" "$DEST"
          git add "$DEST"
          git commit -m "agent log: jira-ticket run ${RUN_ID}"
          git push origin main

      - name: Trigger agent improver
        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
        run: |
          DATE="$(date -u +%F)"
          gh workflow run agent-improver.yml \
            -f agent_name=jira-ticket \
            -f log_path=".github/agent-logs/jira-ticket/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json" \
            -f log_ref=main

            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
            echo "JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY}"
            echo
            cat agents/jira-ticket/prompt.md
            echo PROMPT_EOF
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - id: claude
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        env:
          JIRA_CONFIG_FILE: ${{ github.workspace }}/.jira/config.yml
          JIRA_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.JIRA_API_TOKEN }}
          JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
        with:
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          prompt: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
          settings: |
            { "env": { "CONTEXT7_API_KEY": "${{ secrets.CONTEXT7_API_KEY }}" } }
          claude_env: |
            JIRA_CONFIG_FILE: ${{ github.workspace }}/.jira/config.yml
            JIRA_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.JIRA_API_TOKEN }}
            JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
          claude_args: |
            --allowedTools "Bash(jira:*),Bash(bin/jira:*),Read,Glob,Grep,Write,mcp__context7__resolve-library-id,mcp__context7__query-docs,WebSearch,WebFetch"

      - name: Post Slack summary
        if: always() && hashFiles('slack-summary.md') != ''
        env:
          SLACK_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SLACK_MCP_XOXB_TOKEN }}
          CHANNEL_ID: ${{ vars.SLACK_OBSERVABILITY_CHANNEL_ID }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          : "${SLACK_TOKEN:?SLACK_MCP_XOXB_TOKEN secret is required}"
          : "${CHANNEL_ID:?SLACK_OBSERVABILITY_CHANNEL_ID repo variable is required}"
          payload=$(jq -n \
            --arg channel "${CHANNEL_ID}" \
            --rawfile text slack-summary.md \
            '{channel: $channel, text: $text, mrkdwn: true}')
          resp=$(curl -sS -X POST https://slack.com/api/chat.postMessage \
            -H "Authorization: Bearer ${SLACK_TOKEN}" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8" \
            --data "${payload}")
          echo "${resp}" | jq .
          [ "$(echo "${resp}" | jq -r .ok)" = "true" ]

      - name: Commit execution log to main
        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
        env:
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
          LOG_PATH: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.execution_file }}
        run: |
          set -e
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git fetch --depth=1 origin main
          git checkout -B main origin/main
          DATE="$(date -u +%F)"
          DEST=".github/agent-logs/jira-ticket/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json"
          mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DEST")"
          cp "$LOG_PATH" "$DEST"
          git add "$DEST"
          git commit -m "agent log: jira-ticket run ${RUN_ID}"
          git push origin main

      - name: Trigger agent improver
        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
        run: |
          DATE="$(date -u +%F)"
          gh workflow run agent-improver.yml \
            -f agent_name=jira-ticket \
            -f log_path=".github/agent-logs/jira-ticket/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json" \
            -f log_ref=main

content.text:
### Edit issue

Source: https://github.com/ankitpokhrel/jira-cli/blob/main/README.md

Edit an issue with various options including summary, priority, labels, components, and description. The `--no-input` option disables interactive prompts. Labels, components, and fixVersions can be added or removed.

```sh
# Edit issue in the configured project
$ jira issue edit ISSUE-1 -s"New Bug" -yHigh -lbug -lurgent -CBackend -b"Bug description"

# Use --no-input option to disable interactive prompt
$ jira issue edit ISSUE-1 -s"New updated summary" --no-input

# Use minus (-) to remove label, component or fixVersion
# For instance, to edit issue to
#   - remove label p2, component FE, fixVersion v1.0
#   - add label p1, component BE, fixVersion v2.0
$ jira issue edit ISSUE-1 --label -p2 --label p1 --component -FE --component BE --fix-version -v1.0 --fix-version v2.0
```

--------------------------------

### Create an issue with specified parameters and no input prompt

Source: https://github.com/ankitpokhrel/jira-cli/blob/main/README.md

Creates a new Jira issue with predefined parameters (type, summary, priority, labels, description, fix version) and skips the interactive prompt.

```sh
# Pass required parameters and use --no-input option to skip prompt
$ jira issue create -tBug -s"New Bug" -yHigh -lbug -lurgent -b"Bug description" --fix-version v2.0 --no-input
```

--------------------------------

### Issue Edit Command

Source: https://github.com/ankitpokhrel/jira-cli/blob/main/_autodocs/cli-commands.md

Edits an existing issue, allowing updates to summary, description, type, priority, labels, and components.

```bash
jira issue edit <issue-key> [flags]
```

```bash
jira issue edit PROJ-123 -s"New summary"
jira issue edit PROJ-123 -y"High" -l"urgent,backend"
```

### issue edit

Source: https://github.com/ankitpokhrel/jira-cli/blob/main/_autodocs/cli-commands.md

The `jira issue edit` command allows modification of existing issues. Available flags include `--summary` (`-s`) for changing the summary, `--body` (`-b`) for the description, `--type` (`-t`) for the issue type, `--priority` (`-y`) for priority, `--labels` (`-l`) for setting labels (comma-separated), and `--component` (`-c`) for setting components (repeatable).

--------------------------------

### Issue > Create

Source: https://github.com/ankitpokhrel/jira-cli/blob/main/README.md

The command supports both [Github-flavored](https://github.github.com/gfm/) and [Jira-flavored](https://jira.atlassian.com/secure/WikiRendererHelpAction.jspa?section=all) markdown for writing description. You can load pre-defined templates using `--template` flag.
